twitter.post: It is easy to get stuck in a bubble when reading the news. We added a feature to show you the political bias of different stories. It helps you see the full picture and understand how a topic is being covered by others. HEADLINNE.com #MediaLiteracy #NewsTips

linkedin.title: Most news apps are designed to keep you scrolling.
linkedin.body:
We built Headlinne to do the opposite. We believe that staying informed shouldn't feel like a chore or a constant race to keep up with every headline. The problem with modern news isn't the volume of information. It is the noise.

Our philosophy is simple. We use AI not to feed you more, but to filter better. We want to strip away the clutter so you can actually digest the stories that matter to you. It is about giving you back your time instead of demanding it.

True personalization means understanding what you care about and delivering it clearly. It means respecting your attention. We are trying to prove that you can be well-informed without feeling overwhelmed.
linkedin.cta: If you are looking for a quieter way to stay on top of the news, take a look at HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Finance Stories Today

slides.headline: Big Tech AI warnings grow
slides.explanation: Microsoft's CEO is backing up concerns raised by Palantir's boss about the future of AI. It is a sign that even industry leaders are thinking carefully about where this tech is headed.

slides.headline: Inflation might have peaked
slides.explanation: New York Fed President John Williams says interest rates are in a good spot right now. He pointed to five specific reasons why he thinks the recent price hikes are finally cooling down.

slides.headline: Asian markets take a hit
slides.explanation: Stock markets in Asia are down today after a rough day for South Korean chip companies. Investors are now turning their attention to upcoming earnings reports from TSMC to see where things go next.

slides.headline: The huge cost of influencer ads
slides.explanation: Ever wonder what celebrities make for a simple post? The CEO of deodorant brand Wild revealed that stars can get paid up to 1 million pounds just to promote products on Instagram.

slides.headline: Job ads must show salaries
slides.explanation: New laws are coming that require companies to list pay information on job postings. The government says this transparency will help job hunters and cut down on unfair pay practices.

instagram.caption: From the latest on AI and the economy to the surprising world of influencer marketing, here is what you need to know in finance today. Stay updated with the biggest business stories in just a few minutes. Check the link in our bio to get more news like this daily. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: Deeming drops legal bid against party
slides.explanation: Victorian MP Moira Deeming has ended her court challenge against the Liberal party. Now the party must decide whether to mediate or move ahead with removing her endorsement.

slides.headline: US strikes Iran, Trump issues warning
slides.explanation: The US has launched new military strikes against Iran. President Trump warned Tehran to behave while stating he hasn't decided on further military action yet.

slides.headline: US hits oil tanker in Hormuz Strait
slides.explanation: The US used missiles to disable an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz. This is part of an ongoing blockade on Iranian ports, raising fears of a wider conflict.

slides.headline: Toronto chokes on wildfire smoke
slides.explanation: Toronto now has the worst air quality in the world due to thick wildfire smoke. The pollution is also threatening major cities across the United States.

slides.headline: Kenya court rejects cannabis request
slides.explanation: A legal bid to allow cannabis use for religious purposes by Rastafarians in Kenya has failed. The court suggested the country needs a wider public debate on drug policy.

instagram.caption: From rising tensions in the Middle East to air quality alerts in Canada, here is your quick update on today's top global stories. We break down the news so you can stay informed without the clutter. Tap through to catch up on what is happening right now. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
